meta.title: Conditionals
meta.intro: You warmed these up on paper in Trace the Gate and Write the Rule, hand-tracing which branch runs. Now type and run the same gate scenario for real — the problems get harder as you go, from a single IF to an IF-ELSE with a computed condition, ending with one you build from scratch. Evaluate the condition first, predict the output, then check yourself, hardest last.

problems.prompt: A rider is 52 inches tall. Write an IF that displays exactly 'Board the ride' when height is at least 48. (For 52, it should print.)
problems.promptHtml: A rider is 52 inches tall. Write an IF that displays exactly Board the ride when height is at least 48. (For 52, it should print.)

starter.python:
height = 52
# write an IF: if height is at least 48 (>=), print exactly: Board the ride
# a plain IF does nothing when the condition is false

problems.hint: Use >= for 'at least': the condition height >= 48 is true for 52, so the block runs. Put the print inside the IF so it only runs when the condition is true.

problems.prompt: A rider is 45 inches tall. Write an IF-ELSE: display 'Board the ride' when height is at least 48, otherwise display 'Grow a bit more'. (For 45, it should print the second message.)
problems.promptHtml: A rider is 45 inches tall. Write an IF-ELSE: display Board the ride when height is at least 48, otherwise display Grow a bit more. (For 45, it should print the second message.)

starter.python:
height = 45
# write an IF-ELSE:
#   if height >= 48: print exactly: Board the ride
#   otherwise (else): print exactly: Grow a bit more
# exactly one branch runs

problems.hint: Evaluate the condition first: 45 >= 48 is false, so the ELSE block runs. The ELSE has no condition of its own — it catches every case the IF did not make true.

problems.prompt: A group ticket costs 12 dollars per person for a group of 4. Compute the total, then display 'Group discount applies' when the total is at least 40, otherwise display 'No discount'.
problems.promptHtml: A group ticket costs 12 dollars per person for a group of 4. Compute the total, then display Group discount applies when the total is at least 40, otherwise display No discount.

starter.python:
pricePerPerson = 12
groupSize = 4
# 1) compute total = pricePerPerson * groupSize
# 2) IF total is at least 40 (>=): print exactly: Group discount applies
#    ELSE: print exactly: No discount

reference.python:
pricePerPerson = 12
groupSize = 4
total = pricePerPerson * groupSize
if total >= 40:
    print("Group discount applies")
else:
    print("No discount")

problems.hint: First do the arithmetic: total = 12 * 4 = 48. Then the condition total >= 40 is true, so the IF block runs. A condition can be a computed value, not just a bare variable.

problems.prompt: Build from scratch: a rider is 47 inches tall. First display 'Welcome!' on its own line, then use an IF-ELSE to display 'Board the ride' when height is at least 48, otherwise 'Grow a bit more'. Two lines of output.
problems.promptHtml: Build from scratch: a rider is 47 inches tall. First display Welcome! on its own line, then use an IF-ELSE to display Board the ride when height is at least 48, otherwise Grow a bit more. Two lines of output.

starter.python:
# Build the whole program yourself.
# 1) set height to 47
# 2) print exactly: Welcome!
# 3) IF height >= 48: print exactly: Board the ride
#    ELSE: print exactly: Grow a bit more

problems.hint: The greeting runs before the decision, so it prints every time. Then evaluate 47 >= 48: it is false, so the ELSE runs. Order matters — the sequential line prints first, then exactly one branch of the IF-ELSE.
